title: Egyptian Gratin Potatoes
description: A rich and flavorful side dish inspired by Egyptian cuisine, featuring roasted potatoes in a creamy gratin.
instructions: Slice the roasted potatoes into 1/4-inch thick rounds.
In a large saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the sliced potatoes to the saucepan and toss to coat with the butter and garlic mixture.
Transfer the potato mixture to a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Sprinkle the dried parsley over the potatoes.
If using store-bought gratin, spread it evenly over the potatoes. If making homemade gratin, follow the recipe to layer it on top.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the potatoes are golden brown and the gratin is bubbly and slightly caramelized.
Remove from the oven and let rest for 5-10 minutes before serving.
